title: Branch-and-Bound ATSP Solver
summary: Implement a Python branch-and-bound solver for deterministic asymmetric TSP instances and report certified results across three tiers.
taskPrompt:
You are working on a Linux VM.

## Task Directory
`base`

## Visible Inputs
- Task prompt: `base/input/TASK_PROMPT.md`
- ATSP problem specification: `base/input/problem_spec.md`
- Runtime manifest: `base/input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml`
- Python entry point with task dependencies: `base/software/python`

## Your Task
Implement a branch-and-bound solver for the asymmetric travelling salesman
problem described in the problem specification. Solve the 12-city and 20-city
instances to proven optimality, solve the 35-city instance to at most 0.5%
optimality gap, and write the required JSON report to:

`base/output/results.json`

Use only Python, NumPy, and SciPy. Do not use external optimization solvers or
TSP packages. Do not modify files under `base/input`.
agentMustDo: Regenerate the deterministic 3D city coordinates for the 12, 20, and 35 city tiers.
Implement branch-and-bound and assignment-relaxation based lower bounds without external optimization solvers.
Write a valid `results.json` containing tours, costs, bounds, gaps, node counts, and runtime metrics.

inputFiles.name: problem_spec.md
inputFiles.description: Authoritative ATSP problem statement and output schema.

inputFiles.name: TASK_PROMPT.md
inputFiles.description: Normalized solve-time instructions staged for the agent.

inputFiles.name: pyproject.toml
inputFiles.description: Solve-time Python dependency manifest for NumPy and SciPy.

evaluation: The evaluator validates tour permutations, recomputes tour costs from the deterministic instance generator, compares Tier 1 and Tier 2 to hidden optimal references, and checks Tier 3 satisfies the required 0.5% gap together with an evaluator-side incumbent-quality acceptance check.

classifier.rationale: The task involves implementing a mathematical algorithm for solving the asymmetric traveling salesman problem.
